message:
refactor(ui): consolidate Alpine.js class bindings and add aria-labels in overseer compose ## Motivation The overseer compose template had two minor code quality issues: 1. **Duplicate `:class` Bindings**: The view mode toggle buttons (Write, Preview, Split) used two separate `:class` bindings on the same element, which is redundant and harder to maintain. 2. **Missing Accessibility Labels**: The markdown toolbar buttons (Bold, Italic, Code, etc.) had `title` attributes for tooltips but lacked `aria-label` attributes for screen readers. ## Implementation ### 1. Consolidated Class Bindings (lines 138-151) **Before** (duplicate `:class`): ```html ``` **Problem**: Alpine.js merges both `:class` bindings, but having two is confusing and non-standard. **After** (single `:class`): ```html ``` **How it works**: Combined both conditional class strings into a single `:class` binding using a ternary expression. **Applied to**: - Write button - Preview button - Split button ### 2. Added Accessibility Labels (lines 186-213) **Before** (tooltip only): ```html ``` **Problem**: Screen readers don't announce button purpose (icon-only button with no text). **After** (tooltip + aria-label): ```html ``` **Screen reader behavior**: - **Before**: "Button" (unhelpful) - **After**: "Insert bold text button" (descriptive) **Applied to**: - Bold button → "Insert bold text" - Italic button → "Insert italic text" - Code button → "Insert inline code" - Heading button → "Insert heading" - List button → "Insert list" - Link button → "Insert link" - Code block button → "Insert code block" ## Technical Details ### Alpine.js Class Binding Behavior Alpine.js supports multiple `:class` bindings and merges them: ```html
``` However, this is non-standard and confusing. Standard practice is to use a single `:class` with all conditions: ```html
``` Or for simple ternaries: ```html
``` This commit uses the ternary approach since the classes are mutually exclusive (button is either active or inactive). ### ARIA Label Best Practices **ARIA label hierarchy**: 1. `aria-label` (highest priority for screen readers) 2. `aria-labelledby` (reference to another element's ID) 3. Button text content 4. `title` attribute (lowest priority, often ignored) For icon-only buttons: - Always use `aria-label` (screen readers need it) - Keep `title` for sighted users (mouse hover tooltip) - Make label action-oriented ("Insert bold text", not just "Bold") **Why action-oriented labels**: - "Bold" → What is this? (unclear) - "Insert bold text" → What does this do? (clear) ## Testing Recommendations ### Visual Regression **Manual test**: 1. Navigate to `/mail/{project}/overseer/compose` 2. Toggle between Write, Preview, Split modes 3. Verify buttons highlight correctly 4. **Expected**: Same behavior as before (no visual change) ### Accessibility Test **Screen reader test** (NVDA/JAWS/VoiceOver): ``` 1. Navigate to overseer compose page 2. Tab through markdown toolbar buttons 3. Listen to screen reader announcements Expected announcements: - "Insert bold text, button" - "Insert italic text, button" - "Insert inline code, button" - ... (for each button) ``` **Automated test** (axe-core): ```javascript // Check for aria-labels on icon-only buttons const buttons = document.querySelectorAll('button[title]:not([aria-label])'); expect(buttons.length).toBe(0); // All buttons with title should have aria-label ``` ## Performance Impact **Zero**. These are code organization improvements: - Consolidated `:class` → Same runtime behavior (Alpine.js still computes same classes) - Added `aria-label` → Parsed once at page load, no runtime cost ## Accessibility Impact **Before**: WCAG 2.1 AA violation (buttons without accessible names) **After**: WCAG 2.1 AA compliant (all interactive elements have accessible names) **Specific criteria**: - [WCAG 4.1.2 - Name, Role, Value](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/name-role-value.html) ✓ Fixed ## Breaking Changes **None**. Pure code quality improvements: - Same visual appearance - Same functionality - Same behavior ## Related Commits - `a455bd6`: Simplified project header buttons (same UI cleanup theme) - `98bb6b6`: Fixed overseer send race condition - Previous overseer work: Clean UI redesign, 100-recipient limit This commit continues the overseer UI polish for production readiness. ## Future Enhancements ### Keyboard Shortcuts for Markdown Add keyboard shortcuts for common markdown actions: ```javascript document.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.ctrlKey || e.metaKey) { switch(e.key) { case 'b': insertMarkdown('**', '**', 'bold text'); break; case 'i': insertMarkdown('*', '*', 'italic text'); break; case 'k': insertMarkdown('[', '](url)', 'link text'); break; } } }); ``` ### Markdown Toolbar Grouping Group related buttons for better organization: ```html
``` --- 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code) Co-Authored-By: Claude
